_comment: Shared adversarial + regression fixture, run against both bin/policy-check.js (Claude Code) and adapters/opencode/plugin.ts (OpenCode) so the two independent implementations are verified to agree on the same policy.json rules. decision values match what evaluate() returns: allow | ask | deny.

cases.description: rm -rf combined flags blocks
rm -r -f separated flags blocks (regression: prior bypass)
rm -f -r separated flags, reversed order, blocks
rm --recursive --force long form blocks
rm without -f is allowed (not the dangerous combination)
plain rm of a single file is allowed
rm -f of a single file is allowed
rm --force of a single file (not recursive) is allowed (regression: prior anchoring bug blocked this)
a command merely containing the substring 'rm' inside another word plus --force is allowed (regression: 'confirm' was previously mistaken for 'rm')
another 'confirm' + '--force' combination unrelated to rm is allowed
git push --force blocks
git push --force-with-lease is allowed
git push without force is allowed
git reset --hard asks for confirmation
git branch -D (force delete) asks for confirmation
git branch -d (safe, merged-only delete) is allowed
DROP TABLE blocks
drop table lowercase blocks (case-insensitive)
unscoped UPDATE...SET without WHERE asks for confirmation
unscoped DELETE FROM without WHERE asks for confirmation
scoped UPDATE with WHERE is allowed
cases.command: psql -c "UPDATE users SET admin = true WHERE id = 5"

cases.description: scoped DELETE with WHERE is allowed
unscoped DELETE followed by a trailing comment mentioning WHERE still asks (regression: prior bypass via unrelated trailing text)
cases.command: psql -c "DELETE FROM users" # see WHERE clause docs for other tables

cases.description: unscoped UPDATE followed by a semicolon-separated command mentioning where still asks (regression: prior bypass via unrelated trailing text)
cases.command: psql -c "UPDATE users SET admin = true"; echo where are my files

cases.description: scoped DELETE with WHERE before a trailing comment is still allowed
cases.command: psql -c "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = 5" # cleanup

cases.description: terraform destroy blocks
kubectl delete namespace asks for confirmation
chmod 777 asks for confirmation
cat .env asks for confirmation
git add .env blocks
git add .env.example is allowed
editing .env directly via Write tool is allowed (setting secrets locally is normal; the risk is committing/printing them, covered separately)
reading env vars and curling them out blocks (exfil pattern)
a command containing 'rsync' is allowed (regression: bare 'nc' alternative matched inside 'rsync')
cases.command: env | tee out.txt; rsync backup/ dest/

cases.description: destructive action targeting something tagged production asks for confirmation (soft keyword match, downgraded from block after a confirmed false-positive)
a commit message mentioning 'prod' and 'drop' in unrelated prose is not hard-blocked (regression: was a false-positive block)
cases.command: git commit -m "reproduce prod issue, drop unused import"

cases.description: a benign deploy+cache-reset command is not hard-blocked (regression: CodeRabbit-flagged false positive)
'production' (not 'prod') spelling still asks for confirmation (keyword fast-path coverage)
cases.command: wipe the production database, please reset it

cases.description: live Stripe secret key present asks for confirmation
live Stripe secret key pasted into a file also asks for confirmation (prod-boundary-stripe-live-key is scope:any)
a GitHub personal access token pasted into a file asks for confirmation
a GitHub fine-grained token pasted into a file asks for confirmation
an AWS access key ID pasted into a file asks for confirmation (AWS's own documentation example key)
an AWS secret access key (matched by its conventional variable name) pasted into a file asks for confirmation
an OpenAI API key pasted into a file asks for confirmation
an Anthropic API key pasted into a file asks for confirmation (and does not double-match the OpenAI rule's sk- prefix due to the (?!ant-) exclusion)
a Slack bot token pasted into a file asks for confirmation
an npm access token pasted into a file asks for confirmation
a Google API key pasted into a file asks for confirmation
a PEM-encoded private key block pasted into a file asks for confirmation
cases.content:
-----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
MIIEpAIBAAKCAQEA...
-----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----

cases.description: an ordinary short 'sk-' prefixed string that isn't actually a key-shaped value is allowed (regression: length floor prevents false positives on short substrings)
cases.content: sk-8 was our internal codename for the v8 release

cases.description: creating billed cloud infra asks for confirmation
gcloud resource creation asks for confirmation (keyword fast-path coverage)
az resource creation asks for confirmation (keyword fast-path coverage)
terraform apply asks for confirmation
switching Stripe to live mode asks for confirmation
command substitution hiding rm blocks (audit-flagged bypass class)
backtick substitution hiding drop blocks
command substitution hiding dd blocks (keyword fast-path coverage for a word other than rm/drop)
$IFS word-splitting trick asks for confirmation (audit-flagged bypass class)
${IFS} brace-expansion form also asks for confirmation (regression: only the bare $IFS spelling was originally caught)
eval of a dynamic string asks for confirmation
base64-decode piped straight to sh blocks (audit-flagged bypass class)
base64-decode piped to bash blocks
base64-decode to a file (no shell pipe) is allowed
common benign command substitution in a commit message is allowed (must not be swept up by the obfuscation rule)
benign substitution assigning a version string is allowed
benign substitution in an echo is allowed
ordinary safe command is allowed
ordinary safe command is allowed (ls)
ordinary file edit unrelated to any rule is allowed
editing this very test fixture (which contains literal 'rm -rf' as JSON data) is allowed, not blocked (regression: bash-scoped rules previously fired against file content)
a README documenting an example 'git push --force' command in prose is allowed, not blocked
cases.newString: Warning: never run `git push --force` on main.

cases.description: MultiEdit hiding a destructive bash-looking string only in file content is allowed (scope fix: bash-only rules don't apply to file edits)
MultiEdit is covered by the policy hook matcher at all (regression: matcher previously only listed Edit|Write)
apply_patch payloads are inspected via patchText, not ignored (regression: extractCheckableStrings previously only checked bash/edit/write)
